qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Public encoder chunk endpoint exhausts temporary storage with orphaned uploads
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns unauthenticated temp-file writes in objects/aVideoEncoderChunk.json.php, while the advisory is SQL injection in objects/category.php::fixCleanTitle().

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's vulnerable code or root cause. The only final qualifying finding is an unauthenticated temporary-storage exhaustion issue in the encoder chunk upload endpoint, not the advisory's SQL query construction flaw in Category::fixCleanTitle().
evidence: The advisory describes SQL injection in objects/category.php::fixCleanTitle(); the vulnerable pre-fix code interpolates $clean_title and $id directly into SELECT * FROM categories WHERE clean_name = '{$clean_title}' ... AND id != {$id} ... LIMIT 1 (objects/category.php lines 229-234 in commit 33ea2beec7c21bb20699a376098d62cedda25151).
The advisory fix changes that exact function to prepared statements with placeholders and an int-cast id (objects/category.php lines 229-235 in commit 994cc2b3d802b819e07e6088338e8bf4e484aae4).

The accepted EvoHunt finding targets objects/aVideoEncoderChunk.json.php lines 2-72, where unauthenticated request bodies are written to YTPChunk_* files in sys_get_temp_dir(); it does not involve category creation/renaming, SQL construction, or database reads from fixCleanTitle().
Session artifacts identify public_encoder_chunk_tempfile_dos as the only final accepted qualifying finding; example/ and schema/ are fixture templates rather than session findings.
